name: Genome Surveillance and Chromosome Stability field change check for June 7, 2026

scope.question: What changed for Genome Surveillance and Chromosome Stability since the 2026-05-31 public baseline publication, and does any change justify a public update?

summary: Ran a field change check from the 2026-05-31T20:14:53.931Z Genome Surveillance and Chromosome Stability baseline publication through June 7, 2026. The local trial audit found zero trial-watch items. Broad ClinicalTrials.gov searches found no result-bearing chromosome-surveillance, BubR1, spindle-checkpoint, mCA, mLOY, micronucleus, or folate/B12 aging-intervention registry. PubMed same-window searches found no correction or retraction to the baseline support set. The closest same-window activity was a JCO supplement abstract associating mosaic chromosomal alterations with epigenetic age acceleration in the PLCO trial, plus CHIP, infection, CKD/cardiovascular, periodontitis, cGAS/genomic-instability, NMN, and SIRT2-context papers that remain observational, animal-stage, protocol-like, review-level, or wrong-scope for direct chromosome-surveillance intervention evidence. No public outlook, source, study, finding, activity item, bundle, or coverage assessment update is warranted.

materiality_decision.rationale: The same-window records reinforce the existing human biomarker and clonal-hematopoiesis context but do not add a tested human chromosome-surveillance intervention, functional outcome, disease-prevention result, lifespan result, safety update, correction, retraction, or mechanistic-mediation result that would change the low-confidence human_biomarker_signal baseline.

search_log.summary: The audit found zero trial-watch records, zero missing detail records, zero stale or missing registry checks, and zero open stale watch items for the scoped track.

search_log.query: mosaic chromosomal alterations OR chromosomal mosaicism OR clonal hematopoiesis chromosome aging intervention
search_log.summary: Found mLOY, Fanconi anemia gene therapy, embryo mosaicism, niPGT-A, sex-chromosome, AML, infertility, and aneuploidy registries. None posted a same-window aging-relevant chromosome-surveillance intervention result.

search_log.query: micronucleus chromosome damage folate vitamin B12 aging trial
search_log.summary: Returned no relevant folate/B12 micronucleus aging-intervention trial records with posted results.

search_log.query: BubR1 BUB1B BUBR1 spindle checkpoint aneuploidy aging; genomic instability chromosome stability aging intervention DNA damage older adults
search_log.summary: Returned no BubR1, BUB1B, spindle-checkpoint, chromosome-stability, or direct genome-surveillance aging-intervention registry with posted results.

search_log.query: mosaic chromosomal alteration(s), mCA(s), loss of Y, mLOY, or clonal hematopoiesis terms with aging, longevity, older, infection, or immune; publication dates 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned nine records. Close records included PMID 42232585, PMID 42216432, PMID 41881101, and PMID 41568536. These added CHIP, infection, CKD/cardiovascular, oncology-treatment, and periodontitis context but no chromosome-surveillance intervention result.

search_log.query: BubR1, BUB1B, SIRT2, NMN, spindle or mitotic checkpoint, aneuploidy, micronucleus or micronuclei, chromosome or chromosomal instability terms with aging, senescence, lifespan, healthspan, older, intervention, or trial; publication dates 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned thirteen records. Close records included PMID 42249189, PMID 42238959, PMID 42068909, PMID 41995619, PMID 41980769, PMID 41806657, and PMID 42236084. None changed the direct BubR1/SIRT2, micronucleus, or human chromosome-stability intervention map.

search_log.query: folate, folic acid, vitamin B12, or homocysteine terms with micronucleus, micronuclei, chromosome damage, or DNA damage and aging, older, dialysis, hemodialysis, intervention, or supplementation; publication dates 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned zero same-window folate/B12 micronucleus or chromosome-damage intervention records.

search_log.query: Baseline PMIDs 42156563, 36988570, 34099924, 15475955, 23242215, 24825348, 18469307, and 9230275 with retraction, erratum, corrected-and-republished, or expression-of-concern publication types; publication dates 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Returned zero correction, retraction, erratum, corrected-and-republished, or expression-of-concern records for the eight PubMed baseline supporting sources.

search_log.query: Date window 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07 with mosaic chromosomal, mCA, mLOY, loss of Y, BUB1B, BubR1, spindle checkpoint, mitotic checkpoint, aneuploidy, micronucleus, micronuclei, chromosome instability, chromosomal instability, genomic instability, and clonal hematopoiesis title/abstract screens
search_log.summary: Returned one bioRxiv title match, a Cryptococcus parasexual-reproduction genomics preprint, and no medRxiv match. The bioRxiv record is wrong-scope for human aging or chromosome-surveillance intervention evidence.

search_log.query: mosaic chromosomal alterations aging; BubR1 chromosome stability aging; date window 2026-05-31 to 2026-06-07
search_log.summary: Found one close same-window JCO supplement abstract, DOI 10.1200/JCO.2026.44.16_suppl.10556, associating mCA burden with epigenetic age acceleration in PLCO blood samples. It is human biomarker context only and does not test intervention, functional outcome, disease prevention, or lifespan.

excluded_sources.title: Association of clonal hematopoiesis driven by mosaic chromosomal alterations with epigenetic age in the PLCO trial.
excluded_sources.reason: Same-window human biomarker context: 482 PLCO participants with SNP-array mCA calls and peripheral-blood methylation clocks showed higher epigenetic age acceleration among mCA-positive participants, especially with higher clonal burden. The abstract reinforces the current mCA biomarker boundary but does not test a chromosome-stability intervention or clinical outcome.

excluded_sources.title: Association of infection frequency and incident clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminant potential.
excluded_sources.reason: Prospective ARIC analysis links infection frequency with incident CHIP over long follow-up. It is useful clonal-hematopoiesis context but does not test reducing infections, CHIP, mCAs, or chromosome instability as an aging intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential is associated with increased cardiovascular risk in chronic kidney disease.
excluded_sources.reason: Observational CKD cohort linking CHIP with age, coronary-lesion severity, and cardiovascular endpoints. It does not establish chromosome-surveillance intervention causality or mCA actionability.

excluded_sources.title: A real-world data analysis of the impact of clonal hematopoiesis of indeterminate potential on therapeutic efficacy and adverse events of immune checkpoint inhibitors.
excluded_sources.reason: Oncology-treatment biomarker analysis of CHIP and immune checkpoint inhibitor outcomes. It is not aging-directed chromosome-stability evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Ligature-induced periodontitis promotes Dnmt3a(R878H)-driven clonal hematopoiesis.
excluded_sources.reason: Mouse and inflammation context for clonal hematopoiesis. It does not test a genome-surveillance or chromosome-stability aging intervention.

excluded_sources.title: A dual role for cGAS in shaping cellular and organismal responses to genomic instability.
excluded_sources.reason: Killifish DNA-damage-response syndrome models link cGAS disruption to some rescue phenotypes and some naive-background risk. This is useful genomic-instability mechanism and safety context, but it is not direct mCA, BubR1, spindle-checkpoint, or chromosome-surveillance intervention evidence for this track.

excluded_sources.title: A tumour-host feed-forward loop contributes to the growth of chromosomal instability-induced tumours.
excluded_sources.reason: Drosophila cancer and chromosomal-instability mechanism paper. It does not address aging intervention evidence or the current public chromosome-surveillance claim.

excluded_sources.title: A sustained-release depot formulation of beta-nicotinamide mononucleotide for alleviating chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression.
excluded_sources.reason: NMN formulation study in chemotherapy-induced myelosuppression and model systems. It is oncology supportive-care context, not BubR1/SIRT2 chromosome-surveillance or aging-functional evidence.

excluded_sources.title: An integrated anti-aging framework targeting NAD+ homeostasis, mitochondrial quality control, and redox stability: Roles of NMN/NR, PQQ, and EGT.
excluded_sources.reason: Broad NAD, mitochondrial, and redox review. It does not add primary evidence for BubR1 stabilization, chromosome segregation, mCAs, micronuclei, or direct genome-surveillance intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Effects of AGK-2 treatment on sirtuin-2, oxidative stress and apoptosis in ageing pancreatic tissue of rats.
excluded_sources.reason: Aged-rat pancreas study using the SIRT2 inhibitor AGK-2 for oxidative-stress and apoptosis endpoints. It is not SIRT2 overexpression, BubR1 stabilization, or chromosome-surveillance evidence.

excluded_sources.title: Oocyte-intrinsic aging drives whole-chromosome aneuploidy independent of ovarian reserve beyond maternal age as risk factor.
excluded_sources.reason: Retrospective IVF and PGT-A study of reproductive aneuploidy risk. It does not test a LEV-relevant chromosome-stability intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Comparison of niPGT-A or IVF treatment vs. IVF treatment alone in patients with advanced maternal age, a single-center, open-label, randomized controlled trial protocol.
excluded_sources.reason: Reproductive genetics protocol with no outcome data and no aging-directed genome-surveillance intervention.

excluded_sources.title: AYLo - AutoimmunitY and Loss of y
excluded_sources.reason: Recruiting observational mLOY and autoimmunity registry with no posted results. It is relevant mLOY context but predates the surveillance window and does not test intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Gene Therapy for Fanconi Anemia, Complementation Group A
excluded_sources.reason: Disease-specific Fanconi anemia gene-therapy registry without posted results; not an aging-directed chromosome-surveillance intervention for the public track.

excluded_sources.title: Effect of Culture Media and Culture Conditions on Embryo Morphokinetics and Mosaicism
excluded_sources.reason: Completed IVF embryo-mosaicism study with posted results. Embryo culture media and reproductive mosaicism are outside this LEV aging track.

excluded_sources.title: Validation of Non-invasive Preimplantation Genetic Testing for Aneuploidy
excluded_sources.reason: Infertility and niPGT-A registry with no posted results; not a chromosome-surveillance aging intervention.

excluded_sources.title: Genomic hallmarks of parasexual reproduction in three hybrid groups of the human pathogen Cryptococcus neoformans
excluded_sources.reason: bioRxiv genomics preprint about a fungal pathogen. The title matched genomic-instability terms but is unrelated to human aging or chromosome-surveillance intervention evidence.

notes: Triage selected this as the rank-1 next distinct surveillance target after Telomere Protection and Capping surveillance.
The delta window is anchored to publication event publish-genome-surveillance-and-chromosome-stability-bootstrap-2026-05-31-2026-05-31t20-14-53-931z.
No coverage assessment exists yet for this track; this ordinary surveillance pass found context-worthy same-window biomarker activity but no category-level change requiring immediate coverage repair.

The JCO PLCO mCA and epigenetic-age abstract should be reconsidered during a future coverage assessment because it strengthens human biomarker triangulation, but it does not change public stage, momentum, confidence, or actionability on its own.

The public boundary remains unchanged: human evidence is still mostly observational mCA biology plus narrow micronucleus biomarker movement, while direct BubR1/SIRT2 chromosome-surveillance intervention evidence remains animal-stage and safety-constrained.
next_actions: Continue ordinary surveillance for result-bearing mCA, mLOY, micronucleus, spindle-checkpoint, BubR1, SIRT2, folate/B12, and chromosome-stability intervention updates.
Consider a separate coverage assessment when the track needs deeper capture of no-results registries, JCO supplement biomarker abstracts, or clonal-hematopoiesis boundary evidence.

Create a public bundle only if future evidence changes stage, evidence weight, momentum, blockers, safety, durability, human functional outcomes, disease-prevention evidence, or the causal interpretation of mCA and micronucleus biomarkers.
